summary: Excellent C. Miller‑remastered soundboard captures a strong first set with standout jams like Mind Left Body Jam and a high‑energy Wharf Rat > Good Lovin
review: The show is praised for its powerful first set – “LL Rain,” Deal, Shakedown Street and the short but sweet Mind Left Body Jam are highlighted as gems, and the Wharf Rat > Good Lovin transition is called a must‑listen. Recording quality is lauded as an excellent C. Miller remaster, delivering clear, crisp sound despite only two brief reviews.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviewers gave 4‑star ratings and emphasized strong musical moments plus an excellent remastered soundboard, suggesting a solidly good performance with high‑quality audio.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remastered by C. Miller; reviewers note crisp, clean sound and no major issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as very strong and tightly developed, with smooth jams leading into a high‑energy second half; only minor hiccup noted when Phil fell out of sync before Truckin'.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Starts the Mind Left Body Jam solo; praised for beautiful tone.
band_member_comments.Phil: Seems out of sync during the jam, leading to a quick dissolve into Truckin'.
band_member_comments.Bob: Enters subtly and adds beauty to the jam.
